evidence_citations.excerpt: Maybe if I could just focus on what feels comfortable to me, I could start to feel less anxious.
I just want to feel like I'm doing this for myself, but that anxiety keeps pulling me back.
I keep picturing what my friends will think; it feels like I'm always under a microscope.
I keep feeling like I have to make everyone happy, even though I just want a quiet corner.
I keep worrying I’ll mess up and that will prove I’m not good enough.
I keep feeling like I have to make everyone happy, even though I just want a quiet corner.

transcript.text: Hey, I'm moving into a new apartment and the whole thing feels like a test of who I am. I keep doubting my design work and feeling stuck. It’s hard to imagine getting back into the flow.

You're putting a lot of pressure on yourself with this move, and it's like your whole sense of self is tied up in getting it just right. It's no wonder you're feeling stuck and doubting your design work - that's a heavy burden to carry. Yeah, it can be really tough to imagine getting back into the flow when you're feeling like this. What's one thing that you're looking forward to about this new space, no matter how small it might seem?
I think I’ll have a quiet corner for my sketchpad, but I’m not sure it’ll stay that way once the boxes come in. I worry that the noise and chaos will swallow that space.

You're really looking forward to having a quiet corner for your sketchpad, but the thought of all those boxes coming in is making you worry that it'll get overwhelmed by the noise and chaos. That makes sense, given how much you value your creative space. What do you think would need to happen for that quiet corner to stay peaceful, even with all the chaos around it?

I think if I set up a dedicated shelf for the boxes and maybe use noise‑cancelling headphones in the corner, that could keep it peaceful. But the thought of having to keep it organized feels like another pressure point, and I keep worrying I’ll end up overwhelmed again. It’s hard to see how I’ll maintain that calm when everything’s moving around me.

You're really worried about keeping your quiet corner peaceful, and the thought of all those boxes and chaos is overwhelming. Setting up a dedicated shelf for boxes and using noise-cancelling headphones is a great idea, but I can see how maintaining it might feel like another pressure point. Would you be open to trying a small experiment, like setting up the shelf and headphones for just one day, to see how it feels and what you might need to adjust? Maybe we can break it down into smaller, manageable tasks to help you get started.

I’m not sure I can commit to that experiment; the idea feels like another task that I might fail at, which just adds to my anxiety. I keep worrying I’ll mess up and that will prove I’m not good enough.

You're really scared that trying this experiment will be another thing you might fail at, and that's feeling like a lot of pressure right now. It makes sense that you're worried about messing up and feeling like you're not good enough. Yeah, it can be really tough to take on something new when you're already feeling anxious and overwhelmed. Can you tell me more about what's feeling like the most overwhelming part of this experiment for you?
The biggest thing is that I keep thinking if I mess up, it’ll confirm that I’m a failure. I’m scared that the whole moving and design work will just spiral because I can’t even start a small project.

Your fear of failure is really holding you back from starting small projects, like setting up your quiet corner. It's like this fear is confirming your worst doubts - that you're not good enough and that you'll mess up. Can you imagine what would happen if you could separate your self-worth from the outcome of this experiment, just for a little while?
I get what you’re saying, but I still feel like my worth is tied to whether I succeed. The idea of separating that feels like another hurdle in this move, and I’m not sure I can do it right now.

You're feeling like the weight of this move and your design work is crushing you, and it's all tied up in your sense of self-worth. It's like, if you don't get it just right, you'll be proving to yourself that you're not good enough. That's a really heavy burden to carry, and it's no wonder you're feeling overwhelmed. Can you think of a time when you took on a challenge and it didn't define your whole sense of self - maybe something like your comic book project, where you felt confident and in control?

I do remember that comic book project, but even then I felt like my worth was tied to how it turned out. I still worried about what people would think, and that fear kept me from enjoying the process.

You're carrying this weight of expectation on your creative endeavors, and it's like every project, including this move, is a test of your worth. I can see how even recalling a past success like the comic book project doesn't alleviate your current self-doubt, because back then, too, your worth was tied to the outcome. It's like, no matter how well things go, that fear of not being good enough is always lurking. Can you imagine what it would be like to create something, or set up this new space, without that fear of judgment or failure holding you back?

I keep picturing the new space, but the thought of it all still feels like a test. Even if I try to set it up, I worry that my worth will still be judged by how it turns out. Maybe I need to let go of that link, but right now the anxiety feels overwhelming.

The thought of setting up your new space still feels like a test, and the anxiety about being judged is overwhelming. It's like, no matter how you try to set it up, you're worried that your worth will be tied to how it turns out. I can see how that fear is holding you back. Yeah, it's tough to imagine creating a space that feels truly yours when you're worried about what others might think. Can you think of what would need to happen for you to feel like you're creating this space for yourself, rather than for others?

I keep picturing what my friends will think; it feels like I'm always under a microscope. I worry that if I don't get it perfect, they'll judge me. I just want to feel like I'm doing this for myself, but that anxiety keeps pulling me back.

You're feeling like you're under a microscope, and the thought of your friends judging your new space is really anxiety-provoking for you. It's like, no matter how hard you try to create a space that feels like yours, you're worried that their opinions will somehow define your worth. Can you imagine what would need to happen for you to feel like you're setting up this space solely for yourself, without the weight of others' expectations?

I keep feeling like I have to make everyone happy, even though I just want a quiet corner. The idea of being judged by friends feels like a constant pressure that keeps me on edge. Maybe if I could just focus on what feels comfortable to me, I could start to feel less anxious.

You're feeling like the pressure to make everyone happy is really getting to you, and it's making it hard for you to even think about creating a quiet corner that feels like yours. I can see how that would be really overwhelming. It's interesting that you mention focusing on what feels comfortable to you - that sounds like a really important step in taking back control of this process. What do you think would happen if you started small, like with one piece of furniture or one decorative item, and just focused on making that one thing feel comfortable and right for you?
